description: Controlled vocabulary of materials/commodities the producer universe depends on. The `id` of each material is the CANONICAL token Agent #1 (the extractor) must emit as dated_effects[].sector. `aliases` let the extractor normalize filing language onto the id; `used_in` + `consuming_sectors` are the reasoning bridge from a company's products to a material.

materials.name: Lithium
materials.description: Cathode/anode input for rechargeable lithium-ion batteries; demand tracks EV adoption and grid-storage buildout.

materials.name: Cobalt
materials.description: Cathode stabilizer in NMC/NCA batteries and a superalloy ingredient; supply heavily concentrated in the DRC.

materials.name: Nickel
materials.description: Raises battery energy density and is the key alloying element in stainless steel.

materials.name: Manganese
materials.description: Cheap cathode element gaining share in lower-cost batteries; also essential to virtually all steelmaking.

materials.name: Graphite
materials.description: Dominant anode material in lithium-ion cells; the single largest material by mass in most batteries.

materials.name: Copper
materials.description: The backbone conductor of electrification; demand is a broad proxy for capex in power, EVs, and buildings.

materials.name: Aluminum
materials.description: Lightweight structural metal; smelting is power-intensive, so its cost tracks electricity prices.

materials.name: Steel / Iron Ore
materials.description: The dominant structural metal of the economy; iron ore is its primary feedstock.

materials.name: Zinc
materials.description: Primarily a corrosion-resistant coating for steel; demand tracks construction and infrastructure.

materials.name: Tin
materials.description: Solder is the largest use, making tin a quiet but direct read on electronics manufacturing.

materials.name: Gold
materials.description: Monetary and store-of-value metal with a minor but real industrial use in high-reliability electronics.

materials.name: Silver
materials.description: Most conductive metal; solar photovoltaics are now a major and growing demand driver.

materials.name: Platinum
materials.description: Autocatalyst and emerging hydrogen-economy metal; demand splits between combustion engines and fuel cells.

materials.name: Palladium
materials.description: Gasoline-engine autocatalyst metal; structurally exposed to the decline of internal-combustion vehicles.

materials.name: Crude Oil
materials.description: Primary liquid-fuel and petrochemical feedstock; its price feeds through to fuels, plastics, and freight.

materials.name: Natural Gas
materials.description: Major power-generation and heating fuel and the feedstock for ammonia/fertilizer and most hydrogen.

materials.name: Uranium
materials.description: Nuclear fuel; demand is reviving with data-center power needs and reactor life extensions.

materials.name: Coal
materials.description: Thermal coal fuels power plants; metallurgical coal is a near-irreplaceable input to blast-furnace steel.

materials.name: Semiconductors
materials.description: Finished chips rather than a raw material, but the binding input for nearly all modern hardware; AI compute is the swing driver.

materials.name: Silicon / Polysilicon
materials.description: Feedstock for both chip wafers and solar panels; high-purity polysilicon supply gates solar expansion.

materials.name: Rare Earth Elements
materials.description: Magnet metals essential to EV traction motors and wind turbines; supply is dominated by China.

materials.name: Gallium
materials.description: Compound-semiconductor metal for RF, power, and defense electronics; subject to export controls.

materials.name: Electricity / Grid Power
materials.description: Grid power availability is increasingly a binding constraint on planned capex (notably AI data centers).

materials.name: Water
materials.description: Process and cooling input whose scarcity can gate siting of fabs, data centers, and power plants.

materials.name: Lumber / Timber
materials.description: Primary structural input for housing; demand is a direct read on residential construction activity.

materials.name: Corn
materials.description: Staple feedstock for food, animal feed, and ethanol; a bellwether soft commodity for the food complex.
